rulings.Case Title: JAMES CARGLE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 9, 2008, line 1. DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant shifted the burden of producing evidence based on special interrogatories. The Goseny and Ay declarations create triable issue of fact on exposure. Summary adjudication is denied for the special interrogatories do not address the issues. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES CARGLE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 9, 2008, line 1. DEFENDANT THE GOODYEAR TIRE & RUBBER COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant failed to shift the burden of producing evidence. Plaintiff testifies to ordering Durabla gaskets (Vol. 1 at 504-505) and defendant fails to address this. The DeMarse Declaration, Exs. K & L, is not admissible. It is hearsay, an out of state declaration that does not comply with the subscription requirements of CCP 2015.5. Plaintiff's declaration is admissible and creates a triable issue of fact. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 9, 2008, line 2. DEFENDANT RCA CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is off calendar as moot. RCA was dismissed on September 22, 2008. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPHINE CALLAGHAN VS. DANIEL DEL CARLO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 9, 2008, line 3. DEFENDANTS DANIEL DEL CARLO AND FJ INVESTMENTS, LP'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is off calendar as moot. Notice of settlement filed by plaintiff on Sep-17-2008. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES CAMERON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS RELFECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 9, 2008, line 4. DEFENDANT NATIONAL BULK CARRIERS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of producing evidence based on the factually devoid discovery. Plaintiff failed to present evidence of decedent's exposure to asbestos on defendant's vessel. Neither the Ay nor Ganzhorn declarations are admissible to establish exposure. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: LISA RAMSEY VS. PRANA NINE PROPERTIES LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement And For Dismissal Of Prana Nine Properties, Llc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 9, 2008, line 13. DEFENDANT PRANA NINE PROPERTIES LLC'S Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement And For Dismissal Of Prana Nine Properties, Llc is granted. The record reflects comparative fault on the part of plaintiff and an alternative cause for much of her medical specials. Plaintiff's general damage claim is unrealistic. The settlement meets the Tech-Built criteria for a good faith settlement. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: ALEJANDRO HUITRON VS. PLATINUM MOTOR GROUP, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 9, 2008, line 7. DEFENDANT MUNIR SAAD'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled, 10 days to answer. The letter of April 22, complies with Civil Code section 1782 and plaintiff states a cause of action under CC section 1770 (a). =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: JEANNE SCHOENSTEIN VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 9, 2008, line 8. DEFENDANTS THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND MARCELO SAMAME'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the 4th and 5th causes of action per stipulation. The demurrer is sustained as to the 2nd cause of action without leave to amend. Because Samame is a subordinate, not a superior, there is no factual basis for a claim of retaliation. Moreover, Jones v. The Lodge at Torrey Pines Partnership (2008) 42 Cal. 4th 1158, 1168, fn 4 plainly contemplates that the only unresolved legal issue relates to a retaliating supervisor. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: JEANNE SCHOENSTEIN VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 9, 2008, line 8. DEFENDANTS THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND MARCELO SAMAME'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is off calendar. Duplicate entry. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: ROSE TSAI VS. JIMMY CHAN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 9, 2008, line 9. DEFENDANTS JIMMY JEN AND DELTA DESIGN AND ENGINEERING SYSTEM'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained. Leave to amend is denied as to the causes of action 1 and 2 for there is no private right of action based on the San Francisco Building Code section 103(a) and 103 A.3.1. 10 days leave to amend is granted to allege facts that state a claim under the 3rd and 4th causes of action. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: ALEKSANDR RABINOVICH VS. FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY DBA FIRST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dissolve Or Alternatively To Modify Preliminary Injunction; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 9, 2008, line 10. DEFENDANTS FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY AND FIRST AMERICAN LOANSTAR TRUSTEE SERVICES' Motion To Dissolve Or Alternatively To Modify Preliminary Injunction is granted: SB 1137 does not apply to this loan. Loanstar was not properly served. The beneficiary of the deed of trust is not Loanstar, but Barclays and that entity has not been named. Any request for a beneficiary statement must be made to the beneficiary, Barclays. Plaintiff's request also was untimely. If the injunction is not dissolved, plaintiff will be required to post a bond to cover the outstanding debt or the amount in arrears. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: ALEKSANDR RABINOVICH VS. FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY DBA FIRST
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 9, 2008, line 10. DEFENDANT FIRST AMERICAN LOANSTAR TRUSTEE SERVICES' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained. The Notice of Default complied with the requirements of non-judicial foreclosure. A legal description was not required under CC section 2924 (a)(1), nor was loanstar required to be designated as trustee. Plaintiff may not invoke CC section 2943 against Loanstar because the written request was not made to the beneficiary and the time to do so has expired. Plaintiff has failed to allege that plaintiff did not receive the information required by CC section 2943 (a)(2). There does not appear to be a basis for plaintiff to amend. =(302/PJM/JW)
